channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
item.description: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

item.title: Ever wondered what Mario smells like? Old Spice has the answer
item.description: Old Spice has launched a new collection in collaboration with The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

item.title: La box fibre de Sosh revient avec un avantage bienvenu à l’inscription
item.description: Avec sa box fibre à 24,99 euros par mois et les frais de mise en service offerts, Sosh revient dans le match des offres internet abordables. L’ensemble paraît plutôt intéressant, à condition de bien regarder ce qu’il y a dedans, et ce qu’il manque.

item.title: EA laid off staffers across Battlefield studios to 'better align' its teams
item.description: EA axed an undisclosed number of employees across the game studios behind the Battlefield franchise. As first reported by IGN, EA told affected employees that the layoffs were part of a "realignment" across the Battlefield studios, which include Dice, Criterion, Ripple Effect and Motive Studios. When asked about the report, an EA spokesperson said in a statement that "we’ve made select changes within our Battlefield organization to better align our teams around what matters most to our community." IGN reported that all the involved studios will remain operational, but the layoffs will affect m...

item.title: 'Fantastic, and a disappointment': I love the Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro, but they don't fix the problems of the past
item.description: Samsung's new premium buds sound great, with a range of handy features, and they're pretty good-looking too. That said, they fail to fix key Buds 3 Pro issues.

item.title: The Wild New Theory About Who Billie Piper Is Really Playing in ‘Doctor Who’
item.description: Fans have a lot of questions about the future of the beloved sci-fi series, and the nature of Piper's return is right at the top.

item.title: GM figured out how to navigate EV uncertainty with the Chevy Bolt
item.description: The 2027 Chevy Bolt might not be a groundbreaking new EV, but incremental improvements have made it better without inflating the price.

item.title: Amazon’s Spring Sale starts tomorrow — but you can already save up to 71% on Blink and Ring devices
item.description: From the best video doorbells to security cameras, Amazon has some unmissable bundle deals that won't be live for much longer.

item.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply chain risk designation
item.description: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense on Monday after the agency labeled it a supply chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful."

item.title: Geekom's mini PC deals are live: Some of our top-performing mini computers get big price cuts - but the countdown is on, so act fast
item.description: Mini PCs for business, content creation, and AI all get a big discount but it won't last long.

item.title: Disparition de l’entrée de gamme, suspension des développements : la crise de la RAM frappe aussi la Chine
item.description: Tous les constructeurs de smartphones sont touchés par la crise de la mémoire actuelle, y compris les représentants chinois du secteur. Dans le premier marché mondial, les prix des téléphones sont en hausse, l'entrée de gamme manque d'oxygène et Meizu a même annoncé une pause dans le développement de ses projets.

item.title: Your Espresso Machine Doesn’t Have to Be Fancy to Make Good Coffee
item.description: A former barista explains why your espresso machine doesn't have to be fancy to make quality coffee.

item.title: PC hardware prices continue to skyrocket due to the RAM crisis — but not for OLED gaming monitors, and now's the best time to buy one
item.description: The RAM crisis is disrupting prices and availability of PC hardware, but as expected, that doesn't include the now-affordable OLED gaming monitors.

item.title: Microsoft delays enterprise Outlook switchover to 2027, says move will 'ensure organizations have the time they need to prepare'
item.description: Opt-out phase for businesses to switch to new Outlook has been pushed back by a year, as Microsoft continues to improve the platform.

item.title: Anycubic's accurate dual-color resin 3D printer is perfect for model-making - but I like the $250 saving best of all
item.description: This dual-color resin 3D printer delivers outstanding detail and reliability, and it’s $250 off at Amazon

item.title: PC portables Lenovo : quels sont les meilleurs modèles à choisir en 2026 ?
item.description: Lenovo est une marque très connue spécialisée sur les ordinateurs portables de tout type. Nous avons sélectionné pour vous les meilleurs PC portables de la marque, pour différents usages et budgets.

item.title: 11 excellent MacBook Neo cases to keep your new tech protected and your peripherals neat — from $9.88
item.description: Got a new MacBook Neo winging it way to your front door? I've found the best cases to protect it from bumps and scratches.

item.title: Kamrui Hyper H2 mini PC packs an Intel Core i9 processor, 32GB of RAM, and a fast 1TB SSD into a compact desktop — delivering serious performance in a tiny footprint
item.description: Kamrui Hyper H2 mini PC squeezes an Intel Core i9 processor, 32GB of RAM, and a fast 1TB SSD into a compact desktop.

item.title: Hyper Light Drifter studio workers form union after rounds of layoffs
item.description: Workers at Heart Machine, the independent studio behind Hyper Light Drifter and Solar Ash, have formed a union with Communications Workers of America (CWA) Local 9003. The wall-to-wall unit covers all 13 frontline employees at the studio, which voluntarily recognized the union in February after a supermajority of eligible workers voted for the measure. The organizing effort follows a rough stretch at Heart Machine, after the studio laid off employees in November 2024, then announced in October 2025 that it would end development on its early access title Hyper Light Breaker and cut further staf...

item.title: Amazon fait -35 % ce casque sans fil à réduction de bruit avec 60h d’autonomie
item.description: Envie d'un nouveau casque Bluetooth à mettre sur votre tête ? Le Soundcore Space One Pro d'Anker devrait vous convenir entre son bon confort, sa grande autonomie et sa réduction de bruit. Le voilà en promotion à 129 euros, contre 199 euros de base.

item.title: EDF Tempo : pourquoi les 13 derniers « jours rouges » pourraient finalement passer à la trappe
item.description: Sur les 22 jours rouges de l’option Tempo, plus de la moitié n’ont toujours pas été activés sur la saison 2025 / 2026. En théorie, les abonnés pourraient donc subir prochainement une succession de journées très coûteuses. Sauf si le gestionnaire du réseau décide finalement de ne pas les utiliser. Et c'est bien ce qui pourrait se passer.

item.title: Our Favorite Wireless Headphones Are $60 Off
item.description: The Sony WH-1000XM6 get a noticeable markdown for a limited time.

item.title: Google’s latest Pixel Watches have fallen to their lowest prices ever
item.description: With longer days and warmer weather on the way, it’s a good time to take your gym routine outside. Luckily, Google’s Pixel Watch 4 can help you track all your outdoor fitness activities, and right now it’s on sale at Amazon, Best Buy, and Target starting at $289.99 ($60 off), a new low price. The […]

item.title: WhatsApp : ce petit changement visuel sur Android va rendre l’application bien plus agréable
item.description: WhatsApp change subtilement l'apparence des bulles de discussions pour les utilisateurs Android. La différence est ténue, mais devrait apaiser l'expérience visuelle à la longue.

item.title: iPhone 17e : plus que quelques heures pour l’obtenir à tout petit prix chez Bouygues Telecom [Sponso]
item.description: Lancé au prix de 719 euros, l'iPhone 17e est le nouveau smartphone abordable d'Apple. Mais avec cette offre de précommande, il devient encore plus accessible chez Bouygues Telecom.

item.title: Kick off your Mar10 Day celebrations with savings of up to 90% on Nintendo Switch games
item.description: Amazon and the Nintendo eShop lead the charge for the best deals heading into Mar10 Day 2026.

item.title: Anthropic is suing the Department of Defense
item.description: Anthropic has sued the US government over its designation as a supply-chain risk, the latest move in a weekslong battle between it and the Pentagon over the acceptable use cases for its military AI tech. The suit, filed in a California district court, accuses the Trump administration of illegally punishing the company for setting "red […]

item.title: Anthropic Officially Sues the Pentagon for Labeling the AI Company a ‘Supply Chain Risk’
item.description: Anthropic says the Pentagon is "seeking to destroy the economic value created by one of the world’s fastest-growing private companies."

item.title: Walmart has a massive sale on Apple's newest devices — $40 off the iPad Air M4, $599 MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e deal from $5.50 a month
item.description: Walmart has some of the best preorder deals on Apple's newest devices, including the iPad Air M4, the MacBook Neo, and the iPhone 17e.

item.title: A Quick and Dirty Primer on Aegon’s Conquest of Westeros
item.description: A ‘Game of Thrones’ movie is officially in the works, so here’s everything you need to know about the first Targaryen to rule the Seven Kingdoms.

item.title: Battlefield 6 teams hit with layoffs despite ‘biggest launch in franchise history’
item.description: Even a record-breaking launch can't seem to save developers from layoffs. According to a report from IGN, the various teams behind Battlefield 6 have been hit with an unspecified number of layoffs; that includes staff at EA studios Criterion, Dice, Ripple Effect, and Motive, all of which contributed to the game. In a statement to […]

item.title: 'Consistently well-extracted coffee time after time' — I'm a certified barista, and these are my top 3 Smeg espresso machines
item.description: I test espresso machines for a living, and these three stylish models will bring a touch of Italian chic to your kitchen counter.

item.title: This reader-favorite 55-inch TCL QLED TV is down to its best price since Black Friday
item.description: As of March 9, you can get the TCL 55-inch Class T7 Series 4K QLED TV for $390, down from $599.99, at Amazon.

item.title: Trump Bros Back a Drone Company as the Pentagon Prepares to Spend Billions
item.description: Powerus plans to go public through a merger with a Trump-linked golf course company.

item.title: Nintendo wants its tariff money back
item.description: The gaming giant has asked the U.S. Court of International Trade to refund duties it paid under Trump-era tariffs that the Supreme Court later struck down. Nintendo wants the money it paid in tariffs back—and it’s taking the U.S. government to court to get it.

item.title: A new iPhone Fold design leak reportedly revealed: See it now
item.description: There's a new iPhone Fold leak from a longtime Apple leaker reportedly depicting a 3D model of the device.

item.title: The ISS May Live for a Little Bit Longer for a Totally Predictable Reason
item.description: Congress instructed NASA to not begin deorbiting the ISS until at least one commercial successor is in space.

item.title: Slate Auto changes CEO months ahead of affordable EV launch
item.description: Former Amazon Marketplace vice president Peter Faricy is the new person in charge of the company.

item.title: Live Nation settles government antitrust suit — that probably doesn’t include a breakup
item.description: On Monday, Live Nation-Ticketmaster agreed to settle a federal antitrust lawsuit with the Department of Justice. Eight states so far have indicated they plan to join the settlement, a district attorney said in court, and four others remain unclear on their status. The case alleged that it illegally monopolized parts of the live events industry, […]

item.title: Les 10 smartphones les plus populaires de la semaine sur Frandroid : la concurrence sur Android est exacerbée
item.description: Pour notre top 10 des smartphones les plus populaires de la semaine sur Frandroid, une vraie concurrence féroce se lance sur Android. Samsung, Nothing, Xiaomi et OnePlus se livrent bataille parmi les appareils les plus populaires sur Frandroid.

item.title: The Samsung 43-inch Class Crystal LED TV is on sale for under $200 — a new record-low at Amazon
item.description: The 43-inch Samsung Class Crystal UHD LED 4K TV is on sale at Amazon for $197.99, down from the list price of $227.99. That's a 13% discount.

item.title: OpenAI releases Codex Security to spot the next big cyber risks to your company, promises to 'identify complex vulnerabilities that other agentic tools miss'
item.description: Codex Security is available to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, Business, and Edu customers and will be free to use for the next month.

item.title: The Phone+ : le téléphone français volontairement bête pour les adolescents est de retour
item.description: La marque française The Phone relance un nouveau téléphone destiné aux adolescents. Objectif : zéro connexion Internet et revenir à un simple téléphone.

item.title: Donut Lab says latest test proves its solid-state battery isn’t a supercapacitor
item.description: When Donut Lab first announced its solid-state battery earlier this year, there was some speculation around whether the Finnish startup had actually produced a supercapacitor, which is a much shorter-term form of electrical storage. Supercapacitors can charge and discharge quickly, similar to Donut Lab's claimed solid-state battery. But supercapacitors are totally different from batteries in […]...

item.title: Anthropic sues US government over supply chain risk designation
item.description: Anthropic has filed a lawsuit to prevent the Pentagon from adding the company it a national security blocklist. This comes days after the Department of Defense sent a letter to Anthropic confirming the company was labeled a supply chain risk; at the time CEO Dario Amodei had all but guaranteed Anthropic would fight back with legal action.The lawsuit claims the designation is unlawful and violated free speech and due process rights. “These actions are unprecedented and unlawful. The Constitution does not allow ​the government to wield its enormous power to punish a company for its protected spe...

item.title: A Daily Multivitamin Might Actually Slow Down Your Biological Clock, Study Finds
item.description: New research bolsters the idea that a once-daily supplement can cause real improvements to our rate of aging.

item.title: Xprize founder Peter Diamandis launches new contest to manifest a new Star Trek
item.description: The famed author and investor has launched the Future Vision Xprize with sponsors like Google, Marc Benioff, Ben Horowitz, to fund optimistic sci-fi films.

item.title: Un même jeu, plusieurs tarifs : PlayStation teste des prix différents selon les joueurs
item.description: Sony ne va probablement pas se faire que des amis. Le constructeur teste discrètement sur le PS Store un système de tarification dynamique inspiré du transport aérien. Selon les joueurs, un même jeu peut ainsi être affiché à des prix différents — parfois avec des remises personnalisées.

item.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
item.description: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

item.title: Russian government hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp users, Dutch spies warn
item.description: Dutch intelligence is accusing Russia-backed hackers of running a “large-scale global” hacking campaign against Signal and WhatsApp users.

item.title: Sam Altman says GPT-5.4 is his 'favorite model to talk to' — but admits OpenAI still needs to fix these 3 weaknesses
item.description: Sam Altman says GPT-5.4 is OpenAI’s best conversational model yet, but admits it still has three important weaknesses to fix.

item.title: Live Nation settlement avoids breakup with Ticketmaster
item.description: To keep Ticketmaster, Live Nation is going to have to make some major changes. As first reported by Politico, Live Nation reached a settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case that accused the live entertainment giant of monopolistic practices. Live Nation will reportedly pay at least $200 million in damages to states that were part of the lawsuit filed in May 2024, but avoid selling off Ticketmaster. Live Nation will also be required to make a few changes to its business practices. According to NBC News, Ticketmaster, a subsidiary of Live Nation, will be required to create...

item.title: The Sony WH-1000XM6 Headphones are 'the best of the XM4 and XM5' — and they've crashed to a record-low price
item.description: The Sony WH-1000XM6 Wireless Headphones are some of the best headphones around and they're now back to a record-low price on Amazon.

item.title: Vers un Frandroid plus fort : pourquoi nous lançons Frandroid+
item.description: Derrière nos nouvelles options de consentement se cache un immense chantier technique qui va transformer votre façon d'interagir avec la communauté Frandroid.

item.title: Salt Typhoon is hacking the world’s phone and internet giants. Here’s everywhere that’s been hit.
item.description: Salt Typhoon is by far one of the most prolific hacking groups in recent years, breaching some of the top American phone companies. Here are all the countries that have been targeted.
